title: Deploy Oracle Autonomous Database on Oracle Database@Azure
summary: Multi-AZ deployment of ADB-S on Database@Azure with Autonomous Data Guard. Recommends VNet peering between app and DB VNets, TAC for availability, and ADG standby in a different AZ for automatic failover.

title: Oracle MAA for Oracle Database@Azure
summary: Cross-AZ Data Guard on ExaCS in Database@Azure. Active Data Guard recommended for cross-AZ replication (block repair, app continuity, read offload). Backups to Autonomous Recovery Service.

title: Implement disaster recovery with local and regional standbys on Oracle Database@Azure
summary: Local standby for HA within same region, regional standby for DR across Azure regions. Uses Active Data Guard with Fast-Start Failover. Recommends Full Stack DR service for automated failover orchestration.

title: Implement disaster recovery with multi-region standby on Oracle Database@Azure
summary: Multi-region DR for ExaCS on Database@Azure using Data Guard. Cross-region standby with switchover/failover capabilities. Includes network topology for Azure-to-Azure cross-region connectivity.

title: Implement cross-region disaster recovery for Exadata Database on Oracle Database@Azure
summary: Cross-region DR for ExaCS on Database@Azure. OCI-managed networks for peering (better performance, first 10 TB/month free). Data Guard for cross-region replication, FSDR for automated failover orchestration.

title: Deploy Active Data Guard Far Sync to protect data across Oracle Database@Azure regions
summary: Far Sync instance for zero data loss protection across Azure regions. Synchronous redo transport to Far Sync, async to remote standby. Eliminates performance impact of synchronous shipping over long distances.

title: Implement Oracle Database Autonomous Recovery Service with Oracle Database@Azure
summary: Autonomous Recovery Service for ExaCS backups on Database@Azure. Automated backup management with real-time protection and point-in-time recovery capabilities.

title: Deploy Oracle Autonomous Database on Oracle Database@Google Cloud
summary: ADB-S on Database@Google Cloud with cross-region ADG. Non-overlapping CIDR between VPC and VCN required. App tier should span 2+ AZs with Google Global Load Balancer for failover.

title: Deploy Active Data Guard Far Sync to protect data across Oracle Database@AWS regions
summary: Far Sync instance for zero data loss protection across AWS regions. Synchronous redo to local Far Sync, asynchronous to remote standby. Requires cross-region AWS networking for redo transport.

title: Implement disaster recovery with local and regional standbys on Oracle Database@AWS
summary: Local standby for HA within same AWS region, regional standby for DR. Active Data Guard with Fast-Start Failover for automated failover. Full Stack DR service for cross-service failover orchestration.

title: Implement disaster recovery with cross-zonal Data Guard on Oracle Database@AWS
summary: Cross-AZ Data Guard on Database@AWS for HA. Synchronous redo transport between AZs for zero data loss. Fast-Start Failover for automatic database switchover on failure.

title: Implement disaster recovery with cross-regional Data Guard on Oracle Database@AWS
summary: Cross-region Data Guard on Database@AWS for DR. Asynchronous redo transport for cross-region replication. Planned switchover for maintenance, forced failover for disaster scenarios.

title: Migrate Oracle RAC Databases to OCI
summary: Compares 4 RAC migration targets: ExaCS Dedicated, Exascale, ADB-D, and 2-node RAC on Base DB. ExaCS recommended for full RAC feature parity. Exascale for 23ai-only without dedicated infra commitment.

title: Deploy ORDS with High Availability on OCI
summary: Multi-instance ORDS behind OCI Load Balancer for HA REST access to Oracle DB. Works with ADB-S, DBCS, or ExaCS. Recommends DB in private subnet, ORDS in public with granular NSG rules.

title: Configure Data Guard for Oracle Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure
summary: Data Guard configuration for ExaCS Dedicated. Active Data Guard for read offload and automatic failover. Cross-region standby for DR with Fast-Start Failover for automated switchover.

title: Back up from Exadata on Dedicated Infrastructure to Zero Data Loss Autonomous Recovery
summary: Autonomous Recovery Service for ExaCS backups. Automated backup management with real-time redo protection. Validates backup recoverability automatically.

title: Configure a standby database for disaster recovery using Oracle Exadata Database Service on Cloud@Customer
summary: Data Guard standby on ExaCS Cloud@Customer for hybrid DR. On-premises primary with cloud standby, or cloud primary with on-premises standby for data sovereignty requirements.

title: Learn about maximum availability architecture for PeopleSoft
summary: MAA for PeopleSoft on OCI. Active Data Guard for database HA/DR, multi-instance PIA behind Load Balancer, Process Scheduler on multiple nodes for batch processing resilience.

title: Provision and deploy a maximum availability solution for PeopleSoft on Oracle Cloud
summary: Step-by-step PeopleSoft MAA deployment on OCI. Terraform stacks for infrastructure provisioning, Data Guard configuration, and PeopleSoft application tier setup.

title: Run PeopleSoft ERP and Autonomous Database in an HA multicloud deployment
summary: PeopleSoft HA with ADB-S across cloud providers. Application tier on OCI compute with database on ADB-S. Multi-AZ deployment for high availability with automatic failover.
